channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KRON4

item.title: Austria's new government is stopping family reunions immediately for migrants
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The new Austrian government said Wednesday that family reunion procedures for migrants will be immediately halted because the country is no longer able to absorb newcomers adequately.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and PHILIPP JENNE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court stays execution of Texas man days before he was set to die by lethal injection
item.description: A Texas appeals court has halted the execution of a man who has spent more than 30 years on death row and had been set to die by lethal injection this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal court hearing set in Columbia student's detainment over pro-Palestinian protests
item.description: A Manhattan federal court hearing is scheduled Wednesday in a suit challenging the detention of Mahmoud Khalil, a Columbia University graduate student that the Trump Administration is trying to deport over his visible role in pro-Palestinian protests at the New York City university last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil didn't wear a mask. He's now the face of Trump's crackdown on campus protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When protests over the Israel-Hamas war took root on Columbia University's campus last spring, Mahmoud Khalil became a familiar, outspoken figure in a student movement that soon spread to other U.S. colleges.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States should scale back abortion reporting demands, advocacy group says
item.description: An advocacy group says it's time for states to stop requiring abortion providers to file reports for every abortion.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE, DEA arrest criminal illegal aliens on idyllic New England island
item.description: Two illegal immigrants were arrested in Nantucket, Massachusetts, on Tuesday, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Drug Enforcement Administration agents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One cat's incredible reunion with its owner after the LA wildfires
item.description: It appears the tall tale that all cats have nine lives may be true for a California Maine coon named Aggie. The beloved feline was feared dead for two months after the Palisades wildfire in Los Angeles left her family's home in ashes. But her owner, 82-year-old Katherine Kiefer, held out some hope.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership on Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote, to be sure, was not decisive, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first nitrogen gas execution. The state says it will appeal
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first death row execution using nitrogen gas, which was scheduled to take place next week. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is known about the deadly January air crash between a passenger jet and US Army helicopter
item.description: Federal aviation officials will continue making sure that planes and helicopters no longer share the same airspace over the nation's capital in the wake of January's midair collision, which killed 67 people and was the deadliest U.S. plane crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: TROY, Mich. (AP) — Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan’s attorney general said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida mother charged with murdering her own baby, detectives say
item.description: Detectives learned that the child suffered several brain bleeds and had a large fracture in her skull.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: No evidence Dane Paulsen's disappearance is related to white van, authorities say
item.description: Evidence suggests that Paulsen’s disappearance is not related to any crimes, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI joins search for US college student who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The FBI has joined the search for a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who went missing nearly a week ago while on spring break in the Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried easily wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of a post-election drama have decisively chosen Democrat David Gottfried in a race that leaves control of the House tied.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Dems accused of threatening government shutdown over Musk hatred and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: American college student missing in Dominican Republic didn't drown, Natalee Holloway private eye believes
item.description: University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki's disappearance from a Punta Cana resort should be seen as suspicious, according to a private investigator.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Canada slaps electricity tariffs on New York, Minnesota, Michigan
item.description: This surcharge will affect electricity sales for 1.5 million homes and businesses across Michigan, Minnesota and New York,
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, Fox 5

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Watch the moon turn red during a total lunar eclipse in March
item.description: The best views of the total lunar eclipse on Thursday night will be from North America and South America. Parts of Africa and Europe may also catch a glimpse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
